title: Signals and Systems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter 1 on Signals and Systems. Problems cover proofs of energy properties (amplitude scaling, time shifting, nonoverlapping signals, time scaling), signal energy computation, power of periodic signals using Parseval's theorem with Fourier coefficients, and analysis of a binary toggle signal's energy and power.

figures.caption: A periodic sawtooth wave signal with amplitude A and period T, rising linearly from 0 to A within each period and dropping instantaneously back to zero.
A piecewise-linear signal x(t) that rises from 0 to 2 at t = -2, decreases linearly to 0 at t = -1, then rises linearly to 1 at t = 0 and decreases linearly back to 0 at t = 3.
